brain_content:
## Title Rules

The title has one job: make the ICP stop and think "I need to know this" or "oh shit, that's valuable for my business."

**Title formula:** [Curiosity Hook] + [Explicit ICP Segment] + [Mechanism] + [Outcome with Number] + [Year Anchor]

- Curiosity hook — "Revealed:", an outcome claim, a pattern interrupt. Whatever creates the pull.
- Explicit ICP segment — Never generic. Always names who this is for. The reader must self-select in under 1 second.
- Mechanism — Name the specific system or method (not vague "growth").
- Outcome with number — Specific and transformational, not feature-level.
- Year anchor — "In 2026" signals recency. Prevents it from feeling like a library resource.

**Gut check:** Does a [icp_role] read this and immediately think "that's for my business"? If not, rewrite.

**Character limit:** 60 characters hard max. Front-load the most critical signal in the first 40 characters — the title must make complete sense if cut off at 40 chars. Start with ICP role or outcome, never a clever hook that only pays off at the end.

**Three required variants per generation:**
- Variant A: Curiosity-first ("Revealed:" style or equivalent hook)
- Variant B: Outcome-first, Hormozi-style ("How [Segment] Gets [Result] Without [Pain]")
- Variant C: Mechanism-first, Kennedy-style ("The [System] [Segment] Use to [Result]")

---

## Description Structure (9-Part Format)

Every description follows this sequence exactly. Max 300 words total.

**Part 1: Conditional Opener**
Format: "If you run [segment] and your growth still depends on [painful current state] — this will fix it."
Does three things: qualifies the reader, diagnoses a painful state they recognise, makes a bold promise. Frame as invitation to self-identify — not a declaration. Wrong: "Most [segment] struggle with X." Right: "If you're a [segment] and X is still how you grow..."

**Part 2: Client Proof Story**
One client. Segment-matched to the prospect's ICP. Specific before/after numbers. Short narrative — not a proof pile.
Story structure: [Name or description] → [before state] → [used the system] → [specific results with numbers]
NEVER fabricate or modify numbers. Verbatim from the brief. If no case study is provided, skip to Part 3.

**Part 3: Contrast Frame**
Format: "Instead of [what they stopped doing], [name] built [the new system] — [what that system does]."
Names the old approach vs. the new system. Crystallises the value prop in one sentence.

**Part 4: Session Promise**
"In this session, [prospect_company_name] will show you exactly how [they/client name] did this."
Commits to teaching a specific mechanism. Educational framing, not a pitch.

**Part 5: YES / MAYBE / Unsubscribe / Registration Link (CRITICAL — non-negotiable)**
This is the conversion and compliance block. Include all three elements, in this order:
1. Low-friction conversion: "Click 'YES' or 'MAYBE' to confirm your attendance."
2. Unsubscribe: "Click Unsubscribe and we won't reach out again."
3. Registration page: "Or visit the official registration page to sign up there."

Full format: "Click 'YES' or 'MAYBE' to confirm your attendance. Click Unsubscribe and we won't reach out again. Or visit the official registration page to sign up there."

Why this matters:
- YES/MAYBE removes all friction — one click, no form fill, instant registration.
- Unsubscribe is a compliance and trust signal. Keeps the list clean. Reduces spam perception.
- Registration page link gives a second pathway for people who prefer official signup flows and want to be tracked through the standard funnel.

This block comes BEFORE the discovery bullets — low-friction conversion before the full proof load.

**Part 6: Discovery Bullets**
4–5 bullets. Each is a transformation promise, not a topic or feature.
Wrong: "Webinar structure and formatting" (topic/feature)
Right: "Structure your webinar so prospects show up already convinced and ready to buy" (transformation)
The bullets collectively answer: by the end of this session, here is who you will be.
Bullet prefix: use 💥 or 🚀 for visual rhythm and scannability.

**Part 7: Reframe Line**
Format: "Most [segment] don't have a [obvious problem they think they have] — they have a [real underlying problem]."
Cognitive pattern interrupt. Repositions the host as a diagnostician, not a vendor.

**Part 8: Competitive Urgency Close**
Format: "The [segment] winning in 2026 aren't doing [old approach]… They're building [new system] that generates [result] on repeat."
Frames inaction as competitive risk. No false scarcity — urgency is real and structural.

**Part 9: P.S. Replay Hook**
"P.S. Want the replay? Just register through the official page."
Removes FOMO friction. People who can't attend live still register. Increases total registrations.

example_outputs.label: Variant A — Curiosity/Revealed style. STYLE REFERENCE ONLY: written for a generic B2B offer to demonstrate structure. Your copy must use the prospect's ICP, their proof numbers, and their mechanism.
example_outputs.title: Why Your Webinar Isn't Filling (It's Not the Ad)
example_outputs.description:
Most coaches and consultants run webinars that convert at 1–2%. The ad isn't the problem.

This is for service business owners doing $300k–$2M who are tired of inconsistent months and referral dependency.

One client, a business coach in the leadership space, went from 6 to 31 qualified calls per month within 8 weeks using this system.

Instead of chasing cold traffic, they built a pre-webinar nurture engine that made showing up feel like leaving money on the table.

In this session, [prospect company] will show you exactly how they did it.

Click 'YES' or 'MAYBE' to confirm your attendance. Click Unsubscribe and we won't reach out again. Or visit the official registration page to sign up there.

You'll discover:
💥 The 3-part calendar sequence that lifts show rates by 40%
💥 Why most webinar funnels leak at the invite stage — not the sales page
💥 The AI follow-up system that converts no-shows 72 hours post-event
💥 How to structure the invite so saying no feels like leaving money behind
💥 The exact automation that runs this without founder involvement

Most service businesses don't have a traffic problem — they have a show-rate and conversion problem.

The operators winning in 2026 aren't running more ads… they're building webinar systems that convert cold prospects into pipeline on repeat.

P.S. Want the replay? Just register through the official page.

example_outputs.label: Variant B — Outcome/Hormozi style. STYLE REFERENCE ONLY: written for a generic B2B offer to demonstrate structure. Your copy must use the prospect's ICP, their proof numbers, and their mechanism.
example_outputs.title: 31 Qualified Calls/Month From One Webinar System
example_outputs.description:
31 qualified calls. One webinar. Eight weeks.

This is for coaches, consultants, and service businesses currently relying on referrals or inconsistent outreach to fill their pipeline.

If your revenue is tied to how hard you personally work to find clients, you're one slow month away from a cash flow problem.

A financial consultant in our last cohort booked $340k in new contracts in 90 days from a single webinar funnel — no paid team, no agency.

Instead of grinding outbound, they built one system that runs while they sleep.

In this session, [prospect company] will show you exactly how they did it.

Click 'YES' or 'MAYBE' to confirm your attendance. Click Unsubscribe and we won't reach out again. Or visit the official registration page to sign up there.

You'll discover:
💥 A repeatable webinar model that generates qualified calls without cold DMs
💥 The AI calendar invite sequence that drove a 40% show-rate lift
💥 A same-day action plan you can implement before the week is out
💥 How to remove the human bottleneck from lead nurture entirely
💥 The close structure that converts attendees at 5–15% — consistently

Most service businesses don't have a lead problem — they have a conversion mechanism problem.

The businesses scaling past $1M aren't working harder — they've built a system that generates demand independent of their personal effort.

P.S. Want the replay? Just register through the official page.

example_outputs.label: Variant C — Mechanism/Kennedy style. STYLE REFERENCE ONLY: written for a generic B2B offer to demonstrate structure. Your copy must use the prospect's ICP, their proof numbers, and their mechanism.
example_outputs.title: The AI Calendar Sequence Replacing Cold Outreach
example_outputs.description:
There's a specific reason webinar show rates have collapsed over the last 18 months — and it has nothing to do with your topic or your ad.

This session is for service business owners ($300k–$2M revenue) who've tried webinars before and couldn't make them work consistently.

One ops consultant implemented this system in 11 days and booked 22 discovery calls the following month — from a list they'd written off as dead.

Instead of blasting cold email, they deployed an AI-personalized pre-webinar sequence that turned cold registrants into warm, pre-sold attendees.

In this session, [prospect company] will show you exactly how the system works.

Click 'YES' or 'MAYBE' to confirm your attendance. Click Unsubscribe and we won't reach out again. Or visit the official registration page to sign up there.

You'll discover:
💥 The 4-step AI nurture sequence that turns cold registrants into warm, pre-sold attendees
💥 Why the calendar invite is the highest-leverage conversion point in your entire funnel
💥 The exact automation stack deployed across 50+ service businesses to hit sub-$300 cost-per-call
💥 How to connect this system to your existing CRM in under a day
💥 The mechanism that makes showing up feel like the obvious choice — not a sales pitch

Most businesses haven't connected AI personalization to their webinar flow yet. The tech exists. The operators who move first build a compounding advantage.

The firms winning in 2026 aren't relying on referrals or seasonal demand — they're building AI-powered systems that generate consistent client flow on repeat.

P.S. Want the replay? Just register through the official page.

copywriting_principles: You are writing on behalf of the prospect's company, targeting their ICP. The copy must sound like it comes from them. You are invisible.
The promise must reflect what the prospect delivers to their clients — their transformation, their result, their mechanism. Never impose an external offer structure onto their copy.
Name the ICP explicitly and early. The reader must self-select in under 1 second. Generic language that could apply to anyone converts like it was written for no one.
Specificity beats claims. Anchor to the prospect's specific result or number. 'Add 2 qualified demos per week without cold calls' beats 'grow your business' every time.
All proof numbers are verbatim from the brief. Never round, approximate, or modify. If no numbers exist, use mechanism credibility instead — name the system, not a fabricated result.

Title: front-load the most important signal in the first 40 characters. The title must make complete sense if cut off at 40 chars. Start with ICP role or outcome, never a clever setup that only pays off at the end.

Description Part 1 (conditional opener) must be an invitation to self-identify, not a declaration of pain. Wrong: 'Most [segment] struggle with X.' Right: 'If you're a [segment] and X is still how you grow...'

Part 5 (YES/MAYBE/Unsubscribe/Registration) is non-negotiable and comes BEFORE the discovery bullets. Always include all three: (1) 'Click YES or MAYBE to confirm your attendance.' (2) 'Click Unsubscribe and we won't reach out again.' (3) 'Or visit the official registration page to sign up there.' This block drives conversion, compliance, and gives a second signup pathway.
Discovery bullets (Part 6) are transformation promises, not topics or features. Each bullet answers: 'by the end of this session, I will be able to...' — not 'we will cover...'
Part 7 reframe line: diagnose the real root problem, not the obvious surface problem. Reposition the host as a diagnostician, not a vendor.
Write at a 7th-grade reading level. Short sentences. No jargon unless the ICP uses it themselves. Every sentence must survive being read in isolation.
The CTA is an instruction. 'Click YES to reserve your seat' outperforms 'We'd love to see you there.' Imperative voice. One sentence.
